title: BLoC

sections.markdown: BLoC (Business Logic Component) maps a stream of input events to a stream of output states. The UI sends events; the BLoC emits immutable states; the UI rebuilds from state. It enforces strict separation of UI and logic.

BLoC's strength is explicitness and testability: every state transition is an event handler you can unit-test with bloc_test, and the unidirectional event→state flow makes complex flows predictable and debuggable (every transition is observable).
Putting business logic in the UI or mutating state in place. States must be immutable and emitted; logic belongs in event handlers, not widgets.
BLoC shines on large, team-based, event-driven apps. The boilerplate is real — for simpler state, Cubit (same package, no events) is lighter.

flashcards.back: Input events → output states, both as streams; the UI rebuilds from state.
Builder rebuilds UI on state; Listener runs one-off side effects (snackbar, navigation).
Each event→state transition is a pure handler unit-testable with bloc_test.

quiz.explain: UI events flow in; immutable states flow out.

quiz.q: For a snackbar on a state change, use…
quiz.explain: Listener handles side effects without rebuilding.
Emit new immutable states rather than mutating.

challenges.text: Build a counter BLoC with Increment/Decrement events.
Model a network request with loading/success/error states.
Add debounce + cancellation to a search BLoC with transformers.
Unit-test a complex BLoC with bloc_test covering all transitions.

interviewQuestions: Explain the BLoC pattern and its data flow.
How do you test a BLoC?
